methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cover
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cuby Technologies is a Series B-stage housing and construction company founded by Aleks Gampel and Aleh Kandrashou. The target has raised $23.4M to date. The Housing & Construction sector contains limited publicly traded comparables and a sparse private peer set; two private companies (ICON and Cover) offer directional context, though neither provides sufficient precision for formal peer-median valuation.

Given the narrow comp universe, stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($23.4M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $44M. This reflects standard step-up expectations between institutional financing rounds in the sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $38M to $51M, with a median of $44M. The range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression midpoint, acknowledging execution risk and the absence of direct peer-derived pricing signals. Key uncertainties include unit economics, capital efficiency relative to private Housing & Construction peers, and the applicability of general stage-progression multipliers to a capital-intensive sector. A more precise valuation would require either expanded Housing & Construction comparable data or operational metrics (burn rate, path to profitability, or customer acquisition economics) not provided here.
